<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] PNG assets correctly appear in the import graph as zone members rather than being silently excluded — this confirms sourcevision tracks binary static assets alongside source files.
[observation] [info] The zone has no entry points, meaning nothing in the codebase statically imports these files; they are referenced at runtime via HTML or CSS, which is correct but means static analysis cannot trace their usage.
[observation] [info] Two logo variants (darkmode/lightmode) suggest a theme-switching feature; confirm the viewer's theme logic references both and neither is stale.
[pattern] [warning] Binary and HTML assets that are referenced at runtime via HTML/CSS rather than JS imports create a governance blind spot: static analysis cannot verify liveness, duplication, or correct theming logic. Supplement import-graph analysis with an HTML asset-reference scan to close this gap.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Three files — one HTML shell, two logo variants — form a tight asset bundle with perfect cohesion and no external coupling, exactly as expected for static artifacts.
- This zone is likely invisible to the main web-dashboard zone at import-graph level because HTML and PNG files have no JS imports, causing the community detection to isolate them.
- Keeping logos as a separate zone risks them becoming orphaned if the viewer HTML is ever regenerated by a build step; confirm these are true source files and not build outputs.
- PNG assets correctly appear in the import graph as zone members rather than being silently excluded — this confirms sourcevision tracks binary static assets alongside source files.
- The zone has no entry points, meaning nothing in the codebase statically imports these files; they are referenced at runtime via HTML or CSS, which is correct but means static analysis cannot trace their usage.
- Two logo variants (darkmode/lightmode) suggest a theme-switching feature; confirm the viewer's theme logic references both and neither is stale.
- With no entry points and no static imports from any zone, web-static-assets is structurally invisible to all import-graph-based tooling. Architectural governance policies (gateway rules, coupling thresholds, dependency direction) cannot be applied to this zone — it is outside the governance perimeter by construction.
- Binary and HTML assets that are referenced at runtime via HTML/CSS rather than JS imports create a governance blind spot: static analysis cannot verify liveness, duplication, or correct theming logic. Supplement import-graph analysis with an HTML asset-reference scan to close this gap.

</insights>
